title: oxalis exserta
intro: Oxalis exserta, also known as the sensitive plant or compass plant, is a flowering plant species belonging to the wood sorrel family (Oxalidaceae). Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating various health issues due to its high concentration of oxalic acid and other compounds. The plant is believed to have several health benefits, including reducing inflammation, treating skin conditions, and acting as an antiseptic. Oxalis exserta is often prepared in herbal remedies such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used externally to treat wounds and skin irritations, or internally to support digestive health.
